More Notes: >23% of the global population aged 69+ will inhabit climates whose 95th percentile of daily maximum temperature exceeds the critical threshold of 37.5°C, compared with 14% today
sourcematerial: The global population is aging at the same time as heat exposures are increasing due to climate change. Moreover, >23% of the global population aged 69+ will inhabit climates whose 95th percentile of daily maximum temperature exceeds the critical threshold of 37.5°C, compared with 14% today, exposing an additional 177–246 million older adults to dangerous acute heat....maximum temperatures are already approaching the critical thresholds of 35° C with high humidity and of 40° C with low humidity3,51,52 in different regions of the world under current warming levels1.

More Notes: Additional older adults exposed to dangerous acute heat

"Population Degree Days (PDDs, namely the CDD exposure of all individuals aged 69+) is projected to more than quadruple from the current 203 billion to about 778–1008 billion in 2050, with the bulk of this growth concentrated in Asia (where PDDs grow from about 150 to 800 billion)." - Page 3

"...more than 23% of the global population aged 69+ is projected to be living in climates with TMAX95 > 37.5 C ° in 2050 compared to 14% in 2020, an absolute increase of about 177–246 million age 69+ individuals. Most of these populations will be concentrated in Asia and Africa." - Page 3

"With regards to the frequency of hot days, globally we find a surge from the current average of 10 to 19–21 days/year." - Page 3

"...TMAX95, will increase the most in Europe, going from 28 to 31° C, an 11% increment that exceeds the global average of 9%, under high warming." - Page 3

"Increases in age 69+ populations are observed in all regions, consistent with past studies of global population aging, but they are concentrated in high CDDs levels in primarily low-income nations in the Global South, concentrated in Africa, Asia, and South America." - Page 3

"Note that solid lines represent CMIP6 General Circulation Models (GCMs) median, and light lines describe each GCM individually. These plots allow assessing...difference in mean exposure of the <69 and 69+ age groups is statistically significant at the p < 0.01 level." - Page 3

sourcematerial: Climate change will be the prevailing mechanism of change in more temperate nations of Europe and North America. Chronic exposure to heat is projected to double across all warming scenarios.
sourcematerial: Page 1: The global population is aging at the same time as heat exposures are increasing due to climate change. Age structure, and its biological and socio-economic drivers, determine populations’ vulnerability to high temperatures. Here we combine age-stratiﬁed demographic projections with downscaled temperature projections to mid-century and ﬁnd that chronic exposure to heat doubles across all warming scenarios. Moreover, >23% of the global population aged 69+ will inhabit climates whose 95th percentile of daily maximum temperature exceeds the critical threshold of 37.5°C, compared with 14% today, exposing an additional 177–246 million older adults to dangerous acute heat. Effects are most severe in Asia and Africa, which also have the lowest adaptive capacity.

More Notes: More than 23% of the global population aged 69+ will live in climates with extreme heat conditions where the 95th percentile of daily maximum temperature exceeds 37.5°C, compared to 14% today.
